semanticCore: Laboring, striving hard, straining, working to exhaust, or scratch
primaryMeaning: Laboring/striving hard to meet one's Lord
quranicRelevance: Man laboring toward his Lord (Kadih - 'in-naka kadihon', 'kadhan').

keyDerivations.meaning: To labor, strive hard, strain, work to exhaustion, exert force, or toil
Hard labor, striving, physical toil, or persistent struggle

figurativeOrRareUsages: يَا أَيُّهَا الْإِنْسَانُ إِنَّكَ كَادِحٌ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ كَدْحًا فَمُلَاقِيهِ (84:6): O mankind, indeed you are laboring/striving toward your Lord with a persistent labor, and you will meet Him (inevitable human struggle).
summaryEn: The root represents relentless human labor and struggle (Kadh), summarizing the human condition on earth as a constant struggle leading to a meeting with the Creator.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر السعي الحثيث والجهد البدني المجهد الكادح (كدح)، واصفاً رحلة الإنسان الأرضية بكدح مستمر ونصب يفضي به إلى لقاء ديانه يوم الحساب.
rawText: إِنَّكَ كَادِحٌ إِلَى رَبِّكَ , [Kur lxxxiv. 6,] Verily thou workest, or labourest, (S,) or, workest for thyself good or evil, (TA,) or, strivest, or labourest, in thy work until the meeting of thy Lord, i. e., until death. (Jel.) تكدّح 5 It (the skin) became scratched, or lacerated, much, or many times, or in many places. (S, K.) === وَقَعَ مِنَ السَّطْحِ فَتَكَدَّحَ He fell from the flat top, or roof, of the house, and became much broken [in his skin]. (L.) حِمَارٌ مُكَدَّحٌ An ass much lacerated by the bites of other asses. (S, K.) A wild ass is termed مكدّح because he is lacerated by the bites of other asses. (A'Obeyd.) كَدَحَ 1 , aor. ﹷ, (inf. n. كَدْحٌ, S,) He worked or wrought; laboured; employed himself actively; syn. سَعَى; (S, K;) and عَمِلَ: (S:) he wrought for himself good or evil: (K:) he was eager, and strove, laboured, or exerted himself, and wearied himself, in work, in the affairs relating to the present world and in those relating to the world to come: (Zj:) he toiled, or laboured hard. (S, K.) هُوَ يَكْدَحُ فِى كَذَا He toils, or labours hard, in such a thing, or affair. (S.) === كَدَحَ لِعِيَالِهِ, and 🡻اكتدح, He sought, sought after, or sought to gain, sustenance; or he gained, acquired, or earned; for his family, or household; syn. كَسَبَ, (S, K,) or اكتسب. (L.) ___ كَدَحَ وَجْهَهُ It (a thing, S) scratched, or lacerated, his face: (S, K:) or did to his face that which disfigured it, or rendered it ugly or unseemly: as also 🡻كدّحهُ, (K,) inf. n. تَكْدِيحٌ: (TA:) or كدّح, inf. n. تكديح, signifies it scratched, or lacerated, much, or many times, or in many places: (S:) also [so in the L; but in the K, or] كَدَحَ in the phrase كَدَحَ وَجْهَهُ signifies أَفْسَدَ; (K;) you say كَدَحَ وَجْهَ أَمْرِهِ, meaning he marred his affair. (L.) === كَدَحَ رَأْسَهُ He separated his hair with a comb. (K.) كَدَّحَ 2 see 1. كَدْحٌ A scratch, or laceration, of the skin; i. q. خَدْشٌ: (S, K:) or a كَدْح is more (or larger, L) than a خَدْش: (S:) any mark made by scratching or biting: (IAth:) pl. كُدُوحٌ. (S, K.) Ex. بِهِ كَدْحٌ, (S, K,) and كُدُوحٌ, (S,) He has upon him a scratch, &c. (S, K.)
